options.responseData: ={{ JSON.stringify({ authorized: $('Build Response').first().json.authorized, role: $('Build Response').first().json.role, client_id: $('Build Response').first().json.client_id, reason: $('Build Response').first().json.reason || null }) }}

nodes.name: Access Verification Webhook (DEACTIVATED)
nodes.notes: DEACTIVATED. Server-side token verification for the dashboard. Activate only after the dashboard frontend calls this endpoint before rendering. Body: { token, client_id (optional), requested_mode ('admin'|'client'|'ops') }. See OPERATIONS_HANDBOOK §26 for activation flow.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ admin_user: $json.role === 'admin' ? 'mary' : ($json.role === 'client' ? 'client:' + ($json.client_id || 'unknown') : 'anon'), action_type: 'db_query', input_text: 'dashboard access ' + ($json.authorized ? 'granted' : 'denied') + ' for role=' + $json.role + ' ip=' + ($json.ip || 'unknown') + ($json.reason ? ' reason=' + $json.reason : ''), panel_context: 'verify-access', result_summary: $json.authorized ? 'authorized' : 'denied', success: $json.authorized === true, created_at: new Date().toISOString() }) }}

nodes.name: Log Access Attempt
nodes.notes: Writes to admin_action_log table. That table is added by the Admin Copilot migration (future). Until applied, this node fails silently via continueOnFail — access verification itself still proceeds.

nodes.name: Rate-Limit Response
nodes.notes: Response body written by the webhook responseData expression when this branch completes. Rate-limited + missing-token requests route here.
